reasoning: Both theorems faithfully capture the informal statement. (1) `IsFinitelyPresentable` in Mathlib is defined as `IsCardinalPresentable X ℵ₀`, which unfolds to "`coyoneda.obj (op X)` (= Hom(X,-)) preserves ℵ₀-filtered (= filtered) colimits"—exactly the informal definition of "compact." (2) `IsFinitelyPresentedGrp` correctly encodes "finitely presented as a group" using `PresentedGroup rels = FreeGroup α / normalClosure rels` with both `Finite α` (finite generators) and `rels.Finite` (finite relations). (3) The second theorem's awkward structure `∀ j, P(j) ∧ Q` where Q doesn't depend on j is logically equivalent to `(∀ j, P(j)) ∧ Q` because the Mathlib `IsFiltered` class requires `[nonempty : Nonempty C]`, so J cannot be empty. (4) `Grp.FilteredColimits.colimit F` genuinely constructs the filtered colimit in Grp, and "direct limit" is standardly interpreted as filtered/directed colimit (equivalent in Grp). (5) The universe handling is consistent: with `J : Type u` and `F : J ⥤ Grp.{u}`, `max u u = u` matches the colimit's universe signature.
